Period:

Regulation Hub Intelligence Brief

AI Brief 278 articles across 8 categories

Three regulator investment themes dominate this fortnight: AER is funding a sustained NSW transmission cost-assurance build-out around Transgrid's System Strength Project 2026-31 (Project Owner's Cost Assessment, Non-Contestable Risk Cost Allowance methodology, 2026-27 distributor pricing approvals); CASA is simultaneously opening RPAS-over-people (PP 2609US) and uniquely-Australian structural fatigue AD consultations alongside Part 138/133 surveillance and Bankstown airspace redesign work; and ATSB/AMSA are visibly scaling safety-investigation and crew-welfare enforcement capacity, headlined by the six-month vessel ban of a Chinese-owned bulker for seafarer underpayments and a cluster of E190, Cessna, LongRanger and Melbourne runway investigations. Named clients and counterparties in the news include Transgrid, NSW distributors, China Airlines, Embraer/E190 operators, and the Chinese-owned bulker operator sanctioned by AMSA, with WA Main Roads ($339.9m road safety injection) and Transport for NSW Regional Rail framing the state capex pipeline. Insurance Council of Australia's push on Queensland e-mobility CTP reform and NSW Point-to-Point on-street compliance round out a fortnight where regulator capability spend is converging on cost assurance, safety management and conduct enforcement. KPMG should invest BD effort now in three lanes: a Transgrid/REZ regulatory-economics and non-contestable risk allowance assurance offer ahead of AER's determination; a CASA consultation-response and RPAS/structural-fatigue impact-modelling capability for operators and OEMs; and an SMS/human-factors and seafarer-welfare assurance package for aviation and maritime operators exposed to ATSB and AMSA enforcement. Secondary plays sit around WA and NSW road and rail program assurance, and an ICA-aligned CTP and e-mobility scheme-design advisory line into Queensland Treasury.

Cross-category themes

Across every category, regulators are shifting from rule-setting to active enforcement — ASIC, AHPRA, TEQSA, AER, AMSA, AFP and eSafety are all visibly escalating investigations, bans and penalties — while industry-wide pressure for a 25% red tape reduction collides with new compliance layers (NDIS reform, AML/CTF Tranche 2, DISP, digital asset and online safety regimes), creating concentrated advisory demand around enforcement readiness, accountability frameworks (FAR-style, director, practitioner) and integrated compliance uplift. The cross-cutting opportunity for KPMG sits at the intersection of deregulation impact assessments and heightened conduct/supply-chain/fraud controls, particularly where digital platforms, foreign investment structuring, and sovereign/AUKUS-aligned obligations are reshaping risk for both public sector and regulated industry clients.

Clients in the news — this week

VIC cluster is light this week, with a single but partner-relevant item: Maddocks' analysis of Victoria's proposed Occupational Health & Safety reforms. WorkSafe Victoria will face uplift expectations on regulator readiness, employer guidance and enforcement analytics — a clear pull-through for PEPI's regulatory operating-model and assurance offerings, and a hook into DTF/DGS on cross-portfolio compliance cost. Federal activity is dominated by aviation safety and energy regulation. CASA has opened consultation on RPAS (drone) operations over or near people (PP 2609US) and released a Part 133 plain English guide and Part 138 surveillance update — consultation windows are an immediate BD opening for regulatory design, RIS and stakeholder engagement support. ATSB published multiple investigation outcomes (E190 Cairns landing gear overspeed, Cessna 182 fuel exhaustion, Melbourne runway excursion safety actions) reinforcing demand for safety management system assurance. AER approved 2026–27 electricity distributor pricing and released Transgrid System Strength Project 2026–31 cost assessment, risk allowance methodology and NSW REZ revenue adjustments — strong pull-through for regulatory economics, cost assurance and PMO. ASIC's finfluencer expansion, ACCC's $15m Emma Sleep penalty, NDIA legislative amendments (MinterEllison) and three ASPI Defence pieces (UK naval posture, northern systems, National Defence Strategy 2026) round out integrity, consumer protection and Defence capability themes. Cross-cluster: regulator capability uplift, consultation-driven policy design, and infrastructure cost assurance are the dominant BD vectors — prioritise CASA, AER and WorkSafe outreach this fortnight.

regulator capability upliftconsultation-driven policy designaviation safety assuranceenergy regulatory economicsWHS reformDefence strategy pull-through

  • Australian Energy Regulator — Transgrid System Strength Project 2026–31 cost assessment and REZ revenue adjustments
  • Australian Transport Safety Bureau — E190 Cairns landing gear overspeed and Melbourne runway excursion safety actions
  • National Disability Insurance Agency — NDIS legislative amendments — implementation implications

Public Sector

AI Brief25 articles

Defence and northern-systems capability reviews are accelerating, with ASPI's NDS 2026 commentary and northern-Australia posture work signalling fresh Commonwealth investment in capability assurance, supply-chain resilience and AUKUS delivery risk — all pursuit territory for PEPI. ASIC's expanded finfluencer enforcement and NDIS legislative amendments create concurrent regulator-led demand for conduct risk uplift and scheme-integrity advisory, while NFF/industry calls for 25% red-tape reduction and Treasury budget-pressure signals point to a coming wave of regulatory reform reviews. Concrete advisory angle: position the public sector assurance bench for an NDIS Quality & Safeguards operating-model review off the back of the legislative amendments, and brief the Defence partner on northern-basing capability gap analysis before the next NDS implementation cycle.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Defence and Public Sector partners this fortnight on a joint NDS 2026 / northern-systems capability pitch, and stand up an NDIS legislative-amendment readiness offer targeting the Quality & Safeguards Commission and DSS before the next parliamentary sitting. Flag the ASIC finfluencer expansion to the FS conduct lead for cross-sell into Treasury's regulator-performance agenda.

Energy, Mining & Property

AI Brief37 articles

AER is pouring regulatory effort into the NSW transmission build-out — Transgrid's System Strength Project 2026–31 has triggered a Project Owner's Cost Assessment, a Non-Contestable Risk Cost Allowance methodology, and fresh REZ revenue adjustment proposals — alongside approved 2026–27 distributor pricing and a Retail Code compliance bulletin, signalling sustained demand for cost-assurance, regulatory economics and compliance uplift work. In parallel, the Minerals Council is pushing critical minerals and Victorian gold reform, Property Council is mobilising around WA's $250m pre-sale guarantee and Queensland housing optimism, and Norton Rose flags foreign-resident CGT reforms that will reshape inbound energy investment structuring. NSW's housing-industry roundtable on fuel supply pressures and Dentons' April ESG bulletin round out a fortnight where regulator capability spend and policy reform converge on PEPI's energy, resources and property desks. Concrete advisory angle: stand up a Transgrid/REZ cost-assurance and non-contestable risk allowance offering now, before AER's determination locks in methodology.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Energy & Natural Resources partner this week to package a Transgrid/REZ non-contestable cost-allowance assurance proposition aligned to AER's April methodology, and prime the Property and Tax partners on WA's pre-sale guarantee and the foreign-resident CGT reforms ahead of inbound investor conversations. Flag the MCA critical minerals reform agenda to the Resources lead as a Commonwealth policy-shaping pursuit.

Health, Ageing & Human Services

AI Brief44 articles

Major NDIS reform is the dominant regulatory event: the Albanese government has announced a sweeping overhaul that will move thousands off the scheme and flip provider rules, creating immediate compliance redesign and transition advisory needs across disability providers. AHPRA enforcement is escalating with a warrant issued for a banned occupational therapist and a company fined for refusing to cooperate with an investigation, signalling tougher practitioner and corporate accountability. The Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission has published new leadership and Monitoring Policy settings ahead of aged care reforms, while NSW closed 23 retailers in a tobacco/vaping enforcement blitz and the ACNC's 2025 AIS deadline plus calls to strengthen NFP financial governance amid NDIS/aged care reform create concrete governance, reporting and risk-advisory opportunities.

Financial Services

AI Brief26 articles

ASIC is signalling sustained tech and conduct-risk investment: an 18-month digital assets reform roadmap, a refreshed finfluencer crackdown alongside global peers, and continued individual adviser bans (Morgan, MWL) all point to capability and surveillance uplift inside the regulator. The Federal Court's dismissal of the Nuix continuous disclosure case and the Pure Foods Tasmania $4.5m deferred tax write-down following ASIC review create direct pursuit leads around financial reporting assurance, DTA recoverability and disclosure controls for listed clients. Treasury's cash distribution framework, FSC's digital advice push and ABA's 25% red-tape reduction call open a parallel regulatory-design and operating-model advisory lane, while Capital Brief's stablecoin accountability commentary foreshadows FAR-style obligations crypto issuers will need help operationalising.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the FS Regulatory and Digital Assets partners this fortnight to shape a joint ASIC digital-assets roadmap response offering (legal mapping, licensing pathway, FAR-equivalent accountability design) and prime the Audit & Assurance bench to pitch DTA recoverability and continuous-disclosure control reviews to listed FS and consumer clients on the back of the Pure Foods and Nuix outcomes.

National Security & Justice

AI Brief47 articles

AFP Commissioner Krissy Barrett's AIIA address signals a sustained tech/AI capability uplift across federal law enforcement, with parallel investment cues from ABF's record near-kilotonne illicit tobacco detection program and AFP's $1M+ illicit vape proceeds action — both pointing to scaled data-matching, supply-chain analytics and asset-confiscation infrastructure. Concurrent enforcement on a $1.5M NDIS fraud, Sydney-based transnational scam syndicates and ketamine importation creates immediate pursuit leads around fraud analytics, AML/CTF assurance and integrity programs for Home Affairs, AFP and Services Australia. eSafety's intervention on Roblox and Minecraft grooming risk opens a child-safety/online harms advisory lane tied to the forthcoming social media age-assurance regime. Position PEPI's forensic, data and AI benches against the AFP technology agenda and the ABF illicit-trade taskforce now, ahead of FY27 budget shaping.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the National Security & Justice partner this fortnight to pitch AFP/ABF on AI-enabled detection, data-matching and capability uplift off the back of Commissioner Barrett's AIIA speech, and align the forensic/NDIS integrity team to Services Australia and NDIA on fraud analytics ahead of the next MYEFO funding cycle.

Education

AI Brief43 articles

TEQSA is consolidating regulatory capability via centralised Enquiries Management and a new Student Advisory Panel, while the Universities Accord consultations (La Trobe, Curtin) signal a multi-year capability and operating-model reset across higher ed providers. Enforcement is sharpening simultaneously — Chegg's $500k contract-cheating penalty and the federal 'Real Skills Deserve Real Recognition' VET crackdown create immediate integrity, assurance and provider-remediation pursuits. In financial services education, FAAA, SIAA and licensee principals are pressing Treasury for co-regulated curriculum and faster transition rules, opening a policy-design and AFSL compliance uplift play. Advisory angle: lead with an academic-integrity and AI-assurance offer to TEQSA-regulated providers, paired with a VET provider risk-and-controls remediation bench.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Education and Financial Services partners this fortnight to co-pitch TEQSA and DEWR on an integrity-and-AI assurance package, and ready the AFSL compliance team for a Treasury-led adviser education reform sprint. Flag the Chegg precedent to university CROs as a board-level trigger for contract-cheating control reviews.

Transport

AI Brief36 articles

CASA is opening two consultations simultaneously — RPAS operations over people (PP 2609US) and a review of uniquely Australian structural fatigue airworthiness directives — signalling a regulatory modernisation push that needs technical submissions and impact modelling support. ATSB's run of investigations (E190 landing gear overspeed near Cairns, Cessna 182 fuel exhaustion at Caboolture, LongRanger Torres Strait ditching, Melbourne runway excursions, Jericho R22) plus AMSA's six-month vessel ban for seafarer exploitation create concentrated demand for safety-management-system uplift, human-factors training and crew-welfare assurance. WA's $339.9m road safety injection and Transport for NSW's Regional Rail program signal state capex pipelines where PEPI can position program assurance and benefits-realisation offerings. Recommend leading with a CASA consultation response capability and an aviation operator SMS/human-factors diagnostic.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: Brief the Transport partner this fortnight to mobilise a CASA consultation response offer (drones-over-people + structural fatigue ADs closing soon) and pitch an aviation operator SMS/human-factors diagnostic off the back of the ATSB E190 and Melbourne runway findings. In parallel, line up the infrastructure assurance bench against the WA $339.9m road safety package and TfNSW Regional Rail program for program-assurance and benefits-realisation pursuits.

Defence

AI Brief20 articles

DISP compliance is the dominant regulator-investment signal this fortnight: the WorkSec-AIDN partnership (covered across four outlets) confirms Defence is pushing security uplift down to the SME tier, while AUKUS Pillar II momentum (GME, Tonsley's US-certification-bound cloud platform, Manufacturers' Monthly) is creating a parallel sovereign-capability assurance market. The Palestinian-groups Federal Court challenge to arms-export transparency and ASPI's foreign-interference commentary on universities flag fresh export-controls and research-security risk lines that will pull through to Defence Industry Security Program and Defence Trade Controls Act reviews. Concrete advisory angle: a packaged DISP/ITAR readiness and sovereign-SOC assurance offer for tier-2/3 primes and AUKUS supply-chain entrants, anchored to KPMG's customs valuation and trade-controls bench.

Implications for KPMGImplications for KPMG: brief the Defence and Trade & Customs partners this fortnight to package a DISP-plus-export-controls readiness offer aimed at AIDN members and AUKUS Pillar II entrants, and align the cyber assurance bench to the sovereign-SOC/US-certification wave before Tonsley and Brennan lock in the reference-customer narrative.

Commonwealth

AI Brief40 articles

While most regulatory activity this cycle is Commonwealth-led, several developments carry direct state/territory implications. WA has signed an EPBC MoU with the Commonwealth to streamline environmental approvals via a bilateral agreement targeted by year-end, creating advisory opportunities around dual-track approval pathways and proponent transition planning. The stalled national gun buyback — with states missing the April 1 sign-on deadline — leaves state police and firearms registries exposed to divergent compliance regimes, while Commonwealth automated vehicle laws will require state transport, road rules and privacy regulator alignment. NDIS cuts removing 160,000 participants will shift cost and service pressure onto state health, disability and Foundational Supports systems, opening advisory work on state funding models and provider compliance.

  • Western Australia signs EPBC MoU with Commonwealth The Mandarin · 22 Apr 2026, 03:52 PM
  • At least 160,000 people to be removed from NDIS as Labor unveils ‘unavoidable and urgent’ cuts Guardian AU · 22 Apr 2026, 03:06 PM

New South Wales

AI Brief37 articles

NSW regulators are intensifying enforcement across multiple sectors: Building Commission NSW's compliance blitz in the Hunter targets home-building quality and safety, while Fair Trading's 'Bowser Busters' fuel price compliance operation is active statewide. Significant integrity and governance issues are emerging, including ICAC public hearings into Parramatta Council, criminal charges against a public servant over an alleged NSW Treasury document theft declared a 'significant cyber incident', and NSW Health payroll failures underpaying Westmead emergency doctors. Legislative and policy shifts include new child safety reforms for service providers, red-tape reduction for residential construction, a parliamentary inquiry into commercial surrogacy, and a court ruling striking down NSW anti-protest laws as unconstitutional. Advisory opportunities span building compliance readiness, public-sector cyber and records governance, council integrity frameworks, child-safe organisation uplift, and payroll/award compliance remediation.

Victoria

AI Brief37 articles

Victoria is advancing several material regulatory reforms with direct advisory implications: proposed OHS amendments expanding employer duties (Maddocks), a contested merger of food safety regulators opposed by the dairy industry, and a new Commissioner of Workforce Inspectorate Victoria signalling tougher labour-hire and wage compliance enforcement. The state's new child safety regulator is already facing governance scrutiny, while the Commissioner for Economic Growth role and construction sector 'objective' reforms point to shifting regulatory architecture. Energy regulation is heating up, with the Victorian government threatening to override the AER and networks to remove EV charging connection barriers — a clear escalation of state intervention in federally-regulated infrastructure. Advisory opportunities cluster around OHS readiness, food-safety regulator transition, child safety compliance frameworks, and energy-network connection strategy.

Queensland

AI Brief38 articles

Queensland has launched a state inquiry into federal environmental laws, signalling tension with the Commonwealth and creating advisory opportunities around EPBC reform positioning for resources clients. The Crisafulli government is fast-tracking major coal and gold project approvals while a CFMEU inquiry exposes regulatory capture of the workplace safety regulator and prior building law repeals, foreshadowing governance and procurement compliance reforms. New e-mobility legislation closes CTP and injury liability gaps (insurance compliance impact), while contested anti-hate speech laws have triggered enforcement actions and legal challenges. Executive fraud charges and the Taroom Trough resource push round out a quarter where enforcement, planning fast-tracking, and integrity reform dominate the QLD regulatory agenda.

Western Australia

AI Brief36 articles

WA is advancing several state-level regulatory reforms with direct compliance and advisory implications: tightened tobacco and vape laws, mandatory FuelWatch reporting for all fuel retailers (extending price transparency obligations to mining/regional operators), and residential tenancy bond reforms imposing new deadlines and landlord risk. The state government has signalled water policy reform is coming but has tempered expectations on red-tape reduction in the upcoming budget, while child protection reform and Fitzroy River catchment management plans face stakeholder pushback from Aboriginal advocates. AHPRA's national publication of sexual misconduct findings on 100 practitioner records and the IAG-RACI Phase 2 ACCC review also carry WA-specific health and competition compliance touchpoints. Advisory opportunities centre on retail fuel compliance uplift, tenancy operations for property managers, tobacco/vape retail controls, and stakeholder engagement frameworks for Indigenous consultation.

South Australia

AI Brief29 articles

South Australia's Consumer and Business Services has fined nine petrol stations under the Fair Trading (Fuel Pricing Information) Regulations, with penalties set to escalate — signalling a sharper compliance posture on retail fuel transparency. The state government has flagged the upcoming budget will not deliver major red-tape reduction, indicating regulatory burden will remain steady, while childcare safety failures are driving renewed scrutiny of state regulators administering the National Quality Framework. Concurrent activity includes a federal Fair Work taskforce raiding 18 SA vineyards over labour compliance, AHPRA's new public listing of sexual misconduct findings affecting SA health practitioners, and ongoing pressure for stronger illicit tobacco enforcement resourcing. Advisory opportunities sit in fuel pricing compliance reviews, childcare governance and quality assurance, horticultural labour-hire compliance, and health practitioner conduct frameworks.

Tasmania

AI Brief14 articles

Tasmania is advancing several material regulatory reforms with direct compliance implications: consultation has opened on amendments to the Aboriginal Heritage Act, local government reforms are cutting councillor numbers while raising allowances, new sentencing laws are reshaping victim protections, and proposed firearm law changes intersect with a stalled national gun buyback. Integrity and transparency remain pressure points, with the Government responding to a scathing transparency review, Labor walking back integrity commitments, and overdue RTI reform responses being forced by the cross-bench. Enforcement and sectoral exit risks are elevated by money laundering allegations at Hellyer Mine, mandatory child abuse reporting gaps flagged at Tasmania Police, and the announced wind-down and compensation of the greyhound racing industry. Advisory opportunities cluster around heritage consultation submissions, local government governance redesign, AML/financial crime controls in mining, firearms compliance uplift, and integrity/RTI framework readiness.

  • Government spruiks ‘stronger protections’ as consultation opens on Tasmania’s Aboriginal Heritage Act - National Indigenous Times National Indigenous Times · 01 Apr 2026, 04:00 PM
  • Tasmanian government responds to scathing transparency review - Australian Broadcasting Corporation Australian Broadcasting Corporation · 17 Mar 2026, 06:00 PM
  • Tasmania to pay out greyhound racing industry as it moves to shut it down - Pulse Tasmania Pulse Tasmania · 15 Mar 2026, 06:00 PM
state legislative reform integrity and transparency local government reform industry transition and compensation AML and enforcement

Australian Capital Territory

AI Brief19 articles

ACT regulatory activity is concentrated on Access Canberra's enforcement footprint, with debate over whether illicit tobacco enforcement should shift from Access Canberra to ACT Policing, alongside Access Canberra's expanded multilingual compliance communications signalling a service-delivery and accessibility uplift. ClubsACT is pressing the Territory government for night-time economy reform, foreshadowing potential changes to liquor, gaming and venue licensing settings that will affect hospitality compliance obligations. Federal-Territory intersections are sharpening: NDIS eligibility tightening, ASIC's digital assets roadmap, and parliamentary scrutiny of delegated legislation all carry downstream ACT advisory implications, while a Saturday Paper report on whistleblower warnings to KPMG leadership warrants internal risk monitoring. Advisory opportunities sit in licensing reform readiness, illicit tobacco compliance frameworks, and multilingual regulatory engagement design.

Northern Territory

AI Brief28 articles

The NT Government has introduced a fuel price lock mechanism, signalling direct market intervention in retail fuel pricing and creating new compliance obligations for service station operators. Gambling regulation is under intense scrutiny, with reform legislation criticised as inadequate and renewed pressure on the NT-based Northern Territory Racing Commission (Australia's de facto national online betting regulator) — advocates are pushing for federal takeover, posing material risk to NT's licensing regime. A Productivity Commission-style report has ranked NT the worst-performing jurisdiction on illicit tobacco enforcement, and animal welfare regulators face questions after delayed notification of cattle deaths at a major pastoral operation. Court reforms, a government contract probity review, and WHS concerns at Royal Darwin Hospital round out a heavy state-level enforcement and oversight agenda.

NT dep chief min says Darwin was "lucky" over INPEX emissions - Energy News Bulletin

NT dep chief min says Darwin was "lucky" over INPEX emissions  Energy News Bulletin

Energy News Bulletin
Showing 0 regulators

State-gov priorities

Cited strategic priorities across all 9 jurisdictions, refreshed bi-annually. Every source URL is .gov.au.

Housing supply dominates almost every jurisdiction's agenda, with ACT (30,000 homes by 2030), NSW (TOD, 170,000+ homes), QLD (1 million by 2046), VIC (800,000 by 2034), TAS (10,000 affordable by 2032), SA ($843.6M) and NT (HomeGrown Territory) all running parallel supply-and-affordability programs that create consistent demand for KPMG program assurance, delivery PMO, and social/affordable housing financial modelling. A second shared thread is industrial sovereignty and energy transition — Commonwealth's Future Made in Australia, NSW's bring-back-manufacturing agenda, SA's State Prosperity Project and hydrogen plans, TAS's 200% renewables target, WA's Made in WA / Strategic Industries Fund, and VIC's Breakthrough Victoria — opening BD lanes in business case development, capability uplift for new industry agencies, and grant assurance. Health and human services reform is a third pattern, spanning Strengthening Medicare and Aged Care, NSW safe staffing, VIC mental health Royal Commission implementation, TAS Healthcare 2040 and SA's $2.5B health package, where KPMG's workforce planning, clinical service planning and benefits-realisation offerings are well-aligned. Jurisdictions diverge most sharply on community safety and resources: NT and QLD are foregrounding crime reduction and youth justice reform, while VIC is uniquely advancing Treaty and self-determination, and NT/WA/QLD lean heavily into resources, defence and agriculture as growth engines versus VIC and ACT's wellbeing, climate and active-travel emphasis. Defence and sovereign capability is emerging as a federal-state convergence point — Commonwealth's $330B IIP aligning with WA's Defence West strategy and NT's defence priority sector — creating stakeholder consultation and supply-chain readiness opportunities. Public sector reform and integrity threads run through APS Reform, ACT's Parliamentary and Governing Agreement, and QLD's and WA's refreshed strategic plans, suggesting demand for operating model design, target operating model implementation, and performance frameworks. Long-horizon infrastructure strategies in SA, WA, NSW Metro, ACT Light Rail 2B and QLD's Bruce Highway Council all need independent assurance, EIS and stakeholder engagement support. Finally, wellbeing and outcomes-based budgeting (ACT Wellbeing Framework, VIC Plan to 2051, TAS 2030 Strong Plan) point to a cross-jurisdictional BD play in outcomes measurement, evaluation and data analytics — areas where KPMG PEPI can package a repeatable offer across at least six jurisdictions.

Last refreshed 2026-04-22. Bi-annual schedule; source URLs verified to .gov.au.

Commonwealth

  • Future Made in Australia
    The Treasury / Department of Industry, Science and Resources 2024-2034 (10-year, $22.7B package)
    Program design, sector assessment frameworks, investment mandate design, industry consultation, economic modelling, community benefit evaluation
    Source (2024-05-14)
  • Strengthening Medicare
    Department of Health, Disability and Ageing 2023-2027 (reform rolling); Budget 2025-26 expansion from Nov 2025
    Primary care reform evaluation, Urgent Care Clinic rollout support, workforce modelling, digital health implementation, Medicare integrity assurance
    Source (2024-05-14)
  • 2024 National Defence Strategy and Integrated Investment Program
    Department of Defence 2024-2034 ($330B Integrated Investment Program to 2033-34)
    Capability program assurance, workforce strategy, sovereign industrial capability priorities, AUKUS implementation support, capability reviews
    Source (2024-04-17)
  • APS Reform Agenda
    Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet / Australian Public Service Commission 2022-ongoing (4 pillars, 59 initiatives; Public Service Amendment Act 2024 enacted)
    Integrity and capability reviews, workforce strategy, stewardship framework design, data & digital workforce planning, contractor-to-APS conversion support
    Source (2024-12-01)
  • Working for Women: A Strategy for Gender Equality
    Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet (Office for Women) 2024-2034 (10-year strategy)
    Gender impact analysis, workplace gender equality evaluation, Government Board appointment strategy support, pay-gap economic modelling, consultation design
    Source (2024-03-07)
  • Aged Care reform – transition to Support at Home
    Department of Health, Disability and Ageing Aged Care Act 2024 commenced 1 Nov 2025; CHSP transition to Support at Home by 1 Jul 2027
    Provider transition support, data and digital strategy implementation, workforce modelling, single assessment system evaluation, consumer journey design
    Source (2024-07-01)

New South Wales

  • Safe Staffing Levels in NSW public hospitals
    NSW Ministry of Health Phase one early 2024; full rollout 2,480 FTE nurses/midwives by June 2027
    Workforce modelling, implementation assurance, change management support, clinical governance advisory, rostering system review
    Source (2024-03-11)
  • Transport Oriented Development Program (TOD)
    Department of Planning, Housing and Infrastructure TOD planning controls commenced 13 May 2024; 15-year horizon; 170,000+ homes
    Precinct planning, inclusionary zoning advisory, infrastructure funding strategy, development feasibility, stakeholder consultation
    Source (2024-05-13)
  • Sydney Metro – Western Sydney Airport and Sydney Metro West
    Transport for NSW / Sydney Metro Western Sydney Airport line opens 2026; Metro West completion by 2032; network to 46 stations by 2030
    Program assurance, capability reviews, cost estimation reviews, land use integration, precinct economic analysis
    Source (2024-06-01)
  • Bringing back manufacturing to NSW (state-owned energy and rollingstock)
    Cabinet Office / Treasury NSW / Transport for NSW 2023-2027+ (current Parliament)
    Industrial strategy advisory, local content analysis, state-owned corporation establishment, procurement strategy, workforce development
    Source (2024-09-01)
  • NSW Budget 2025-26 priorities (essential services + diversified economy)
    NSW Treasury 2025-26 forward estimates to 2028-29
    Fiscal strategy advisory, expenditure review, program evaluation, revenue analysis, investment prioritisation
    Source (2025-06-24)
  • Safer Pathway – domestic and family violence response
    Department of Communities and Justice Statewide rollout ongoing; DFV reforms 2024-ongoing
    Service design, outcomes evaluation, risk assessment framework review, cross-agency data sharing advisory, program logic review
    Source (2024-01-01)

  • Victoria's Housing Statement – The Decade Ahead 2024-2034
    Department of Transport and Planning 2024-2034 (800,000 homes / 80,000 per year target)
    Planning reform advisory, activity centre masterplans, affordable housing economics, surplus land unlocking, stakeholder consultation, LGA target negotiations
    Source (2023-09-20)
  • Plan for Victoria (long-term plan to 2051)
    Department of Transport and Planning Released Feb 2025; horizon to 2051 (2.24 million homes target)
    LGA housing target analysis, settlement pattern modelling, sustainability and climate policy integration, First Peoples self-determination in planning, infrastructure sequencing
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Mental Health and Wellbeing reform (Royal Commission implementation)
    Department of Health 2021-2031 (decade of reform); Statewide Service and Capital Plan 2024-2037
    Service design, capital planning, workforce strategy, outcomes evaluation, lived experience engagement design, Mental Health Levy economic analysis
    Source (2024-07-01)
  • Treaty for Victoria / First Peoples' self-determination
    First Peoples – State Relations Statewide Treaty Bill 2025 introduced; negotiations ongoing
    Closing the Gap accountability framework design, service commissioning transition, policy impact analysis, governance advisory for new representative body
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Breakthrough Victoria Fund – research and innovation commercialisation
    Department of Jobs, Skills, Industry and Regions 2020-2030 ($2B 10-year fund; $1.3B co-investment leveraged by 2024)
    Commercialisation strategy, innovation ecosystem review, investment thesis advisory, co-investment deal structuring, industry sector analysis
    Source (2024-06-30)

Queensland

  • Making Queensland Safer – adult crime, adult time youth justice reform
    Department of Youth Justice and Victim Support (renamed 1 Nov 2024) Making Queensland Safer Act 2024 assented 13 Dec 2024; Queensland Youth Justice Strategy 2024-2028
    Program logic review, early intervention evaluation, victim advocacy service design, circuit breaker sentencing assurance, regional reset program evaluation
    Source (2024-08-01)
  • Homes for Queenslanders housing plan
    Department of Housing and Public Works 2024-2046 (1 million new homes by 2046; 53,500 social homes)
    Housing target modelling, modular housing economics, social housing procurement, church/charity land strategy, planning reform advisory
    Source (2024-02-14)
  • A Fresh Start – cost-of-living and tax relief
    Queensland Treasury 100 Day Plan completed early 2025; ongoing measures 2024-2028
    Revenue impact modelling, cost-of-living measure evaluation, payroll tax analysis, first home buyer stamp duty modelling
    Source (2025-02-01)
  • Queensland Resources Industry Development Plan (QRIDP)
    Department of Natural Resources and Mines, Manufacturing and Regional and Rural Development 2022-2052 (30-year vision; living document)
    Critical minerals strategy, decarbonisation roadmaps, regional community transition planning, ESG performance frameworks, royalty policy analysis
    Source (2024-10-01)
  • Bruce Highway Advisory Council and regional infrastructure
    Department of Transport and Main Roads Re-established 100 Day Plan period (late 2024); ongoing through current term
    Regional corridor prioritisation, cost-benefit analysis, safety-focused project business cases, stakeholder engagement with regional councils
    Source (2024-11-30)
  • Department of the Premier and Cabinet Strategic Plan 2024-2028
    Department of the Premier and Cabinet (Queensland) 2024-2028 (current through 30 April 2025 review; superseded by 2025-2029 plan)
    Central agency capability review, Cabinet support modernisation, policy quality uplift, intergovernmental strategy, corporate excellence frameworks
    Source (2024-06-28)

  • WA Government Priorities 2025-2029 (Jobs, Health, Housing, Community, Environment, Infrastructure and Services)
    Department of the Premier and Cabinet (WA) 2025-2029 (current parliamentary term)
    Cross-agency delivery coordination, outcomes-based budgeting advisory, capability reviews for new departments, program assurance
    Source (2025-03-01)
  • Made in WA Plan – $1B Strategic Industries Fund
    Department of Jobs, Tourism, Science and Innovation 2025-2035 ($500M additional investment; SIF total $1B over 10 years)
    Industrial policy advisory, biomedical precinct economics, battery manufacturing supply chain, EV bus and ferry procurement, screen industry strategy

  • Defence West: WA Defence and Defence Industry Strategy 2025
    Department of Jobs, Tourism, Science and Innovation / Defence West 2025-2040 (15-year strategic vision)
    AUKUS readiness, continuous naval shipbuilding industrial strategy, defence workforce planning, supplier diversification, sovereign capability analysis

  • Diversify WA: 2024 Update (economic development framework)
    Department of Jobs, Tourism, Science and Innovation Launched 2019; updated 2024; nine external-facing sectors
    Sector prioritisation, investment attraction strategy, comparative advantage analysis, export market development, regulatory environment review

  • State Infrastructure Strategy (Infrastructure WA)
    Infrastructure WA 20-year strategy; IWA Annual Report and Government Response
    Infrastructure business case review, prioritisation methodology, capital program assurance, decarbonisation of infrastructure, asset recycling analysis
    Source (2024-01-01)

  • State Prosperity Project
    Department of the Premier and Cabinet (SA) / Department for Energy and Mining Announced 2023; long-term (renewables, critical minerals, green manufacturing)

    Source (2023-09-01)

    Department for Environment and Water / Department for Energy and Mining 2024-2030 (100% net renewable electricity by 2027; net zero by 2050)

    Business case assurance, infrastructure prioritisation methodology, asset management strategy, capital program reviews

  • 2030 Strong Plan for Tasmania's Future

  • Long-Term Plan for Healthcare in Tasmania 2040 / Our Healthcare Future
    Department of Health (Tasmania) 2040 horizon; Our Healthcare Future released December 2022 with 6 action areas

    Department of State Growth / ReCFIT (Renewables, Climate and Future Industries Tasmania) 2020-2040 (200% renewable generation; Bell Bay green hydrogen hub by 2030)

  • Parliamentary and Governing Agreement for the 10th ACT Legislative Assembly

  • Rebuilding the Economy: Northern Territory Economic Strategy 2025
